transcript.clean_text: It's stage three of Tirreno-Adriatico and it's a monster. 239 rolling kilometers from Follonica on the coast inland to a very, very hard finish here at the finish line in Trevi. And the flag dropped and the usual suspects went on the attack. Eventually, a five-man breakaway establishing itself pretty early on. Dennis Van Winden joining Stepan Kurianov from Gazprom-RusVelo, but Giuli from Nippo-Vini Fantini and a teammate of his, the Japanese rider Sho Hatsuyama. Also from Fantini and Jacopo Mosca from Wilier Triestina. And it is uh Kurianov who's going to be a problem for Mosca. Mosca closes the gap and uh leaves it a little bit too late. There was a line in the tarmac there which I think must have been the intermediate sprint. So, Mosca's going to have to settle for second place. There we go. A spill towards the back. Who's been caught up in that? One of the Mitchelton-Scott riders that is Michael Hepburn. A rider on the ground so race It's the race leader. It is indeed uh Patrick Bevin himself from BMC who's come to grief. So, something must have happened right towards the front there. Patrick Bevin in the leader's jersey on a world tour race, a famous race like Tirreno-Adriatico. Not the way that he would have imagined that he'd be losing the jersey. With 30 km to go and another little spill on the right-hand side of the road. And again, Mitchelton-Scott are inconvenienced. So, two of Team Sky with one or two riders. And uh oh, Sunweb rider's gone down. That's Simon Geschke. That's the move from Jacopo Mosca. There's Kurianov exactly where he needed to be. But is he going to be able to reverse this? He comes almost level with the Italian but not quite. I think Mosca is going to get his revenge for that what he perceived to be treachery when the young Russian stole first place from him as he saw it at the first intermediate sprint. But it's a complete reversal over the line in Montefalcione as Mosca picks up another five points. But certainly not good news for Rafał Majka. They see there's a lot of wheels, a lot of gaps opened up. Well, I'm just wondering there were two Bora-Hansgrohe riders in that incident with Simon Geschke. Buchmann, the rider in front of Majka was definitely one of them. So, I'm thinking that Rafał Majka might have been in that little involved in that little spill. The lead group just gets split. Yep. Battaglin and Van Winden the best of the breakaway on this climb. The attack comes out of the pack and that's uh Edoardo Zardini from Wilier Triestina. Won't be too long before we see him in our commentary position here as Zardini gets to and passes him. And they've still got 500 mostly very steep meters. He is just coming over the finishing line now, Rafał Majka. And he's out of contention and he's also out of general classification contention. Primož Roglič goes on the attack. Look like the move is being tracked by Davide Villella, the climber, the Italian from Astana. As Van Avermaet goes, and just might Gianni Moscon look like he was twitchy as well. So, this is trouble now for Primož Roglič. Can he hold the power all the way to the finish line? Another steep section now. They're in the barriers. Van Avermaet sees himself countered and turned by Gianni Moscon. Thomas still sitting on the wheel of the Italian. Goes past Van Avermaet. Adam Yates now from Mitchelton-Scott in the yellow jersey comes up alongside Thomas. Past Thomas. Alongside Moscon. Past Moscon. Adam Yates now countering. And look how close they are to Primož Roglič. Not far from the finish line now. We're on to the uh cable cameras. Roglič just needs one more right-hand turn. But has Yates left it too late? Roglič such a powerful rider. Dumoulin is still there. Look at that gap. Is it closable? Yates will still believe. He can see that yellow jersey and those black shorts. That's the target he has to aim at, Adam Yates. And he is uh flying away from the rest of the race, clear into second position. One more big sustained effort from Primož Roglič on this climb as Tiesj Benoot comes into view IN THIRD PLACE. ADAM YATES, CAN HE GET THERE? NO, ROGLIč IS GOING TO HOLD ON. Roglič is going to take stage three of Tirreno-Adriatico. A brilliant, powerful ride. Adam Yates comes in second place. Tiesj Benoot picking up third place as uh Geraint Thomas has to settle for fourth. He's out of the bonus seconds. Primož Roglič, well, that was brilliant. I thought he might have gone too early with that highly talented field trying to hunt him down. But he held them off. Just three seconds ahead of Adam Yates. Tiesj Benoot, the winner the other day in Strade Bianche. A further three seconds back at six seconds. Van Avermaet and Thomas locked together on the same time as you rightly said, it's gone to countback and Thomas's fourth place there does the deal for Geraint Thomas and he moves into the top of the general classification.
